title: A Canticle To Apollo
content:
Play, Phoebus, on thy lute,
And we will sit all mute;
By listening to thy lyre,
That sets all ears on fire.

Hark, hark! the God does play!
And as he leads the way
Through heaven, the very spheres,
As men, turn all to ears!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "A Canticle To Apollo"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
